_comment_purpose: Baseline floor for test/bench-regression-check.js. Numbers represent the SLOWEST acceptable run on the configured runner. CI fails if measured > value * (1 + tolerance_pct / 100).
_comment_seeding: These initial values are conservative seeds derived from a macOS arm64 measurement (first_run=225ms, second_run=6ms) scaled ~5x for ubuntu-22.04 GitHub-hosted runners with extra slack for first-run variance. After the first real bench.yml run, update these via PR with the measurement run ID linked in the description.
_comment_update_policy: DO NOT lower these silently. Every change must (a) link a successful bench.yml workflow run id and (b) explain why the new value is the right baseline.

self_repo_first_run_ms._note: Cold sync of ~70-file carto repo. ~225ms on M-series; allow 1500ms*1.5=2250ms ceiling on Linux runner.

self_repo_second_run_ms._note: Warm sync (mtime+size cache hits). Should be tens of ms; tolerance is generous because absolute numbers are tiny so percentage swings exaggerate.

mcp_get_structure_ms._note: Single MCP query. <1ms on M-series. Linux runner with cold SQLite + GH Actions noise can hit 5-15ms; the 200% tolerance lets it run 30ms before failing.
